mnemonics.title: TYPEDEF REMEMBER
mnemonics.content: TYPEDEF: Think of it as TYPE (giving a) DEFINITION an alias. It does NOT create a new type, just gives a new NAME to an existing type.

mnemonics.title: TYPEDEF vs DEFINE
mnemonics.content: TYPEDEF = TYPESAFE (compiler checks), DEFINED = DANGEROUS (text substitution only). ALWAYS prefer typedef for type aliases.

mnemonics.title: STRUCT WITH TYPEDEF
mnemonics.content: STRUCT + TYPEDEF = SIMPLE. Write struct definition, add typedef at start, use simple name everywhere. No more writing 'struct' repeatedly!

acronyms.expansion: TYPE DEFinition - creates an alias for existing type definition
acronyms.usage: typedef int Integer; creates Integer as alias for int

acronyms.expansion: Abstract Data Type - hides implementation using typedef
acronyms.usage: typedef struct* Stack; hides Stack implementation details

acronyms.expansion: Application Programming Interface - typedef often used in APIs for portable types
acronyms.usage: typedef int32_t ApiInt; ensures consistent 32-bit integer across platforms

visualTips.concept: typedef creates ALIAS not NEW type
visualTips.visualization: Draw an arrow from 'INTEGER' pointing to 'int' - shows it's the SAME type, just with a different name. Both point to identical memory.
Write typedef int* INT_PTR; then show INT_PTR p1, p2; Draw boxes for p1 and p2 with * inside to show both are pointers.
Pattern: typedef [EXISTING] [NEW]; Like: typedef int INTEGER. Think of it as 'existing becomes new'.

analogies.analogy: typedef is like giving someone a nickname. The person (original type) still exists with all their properties, but you can also call them by the nickname (typedef name). They're the same person!
typedef is like creating a shortcut on your desktop. The original file (type) stays in its location, but you can access it quickly using the shortcut (typedef). Same content, different name.
Function pointer typedef is like saving in your a contact phone. Instead of remembering the full phone number (complex function signature), you just save it under a name and dial using that name.
